ai_research.headline: Meta debuts Muse Spark, Anthropic previews 10T-param Mythos 5, Microsoft ships 3 new multimodal models

items.title: Meta Muse Spark — first proprietary model from Superintelligence Lab
items.body: Meta's new Muse Spark, built by their Superintelligence Lab (post-$14B Alexandr Wang deal), is small and fast but punches above its weight in reasoning, science, math, and health. Optimized for Facebook/Instagram/WhatsApp. Outperforms all prior Llama models and ranks just behind Anthropic and OpenAI on benchmarks. This is Meta going proprietary after years of open-source.

items.title: Anthropic previews Mythos 5 — 10 trillion parameters, won't release fully
items.body: Anthropic showed off Mythos 5, a 10T-parameter model built for advanced cybersecurity. They confirmed they won't release the full version publicly due to misuse risk — it's their most capable model ever. This is the strongest signal yet that frontier models are outpacing what's safe to ship. Safety debate is heating up.

items.title: Microsoft ships MAI-Transcribe, MAI-Voice, MAI-Image-2
items.body: Microsoft released three foundational multimodal models via Microsoft Foundry — speech-to-text, voice generation, and image generation. Priced to undercut OpenAI and Google. Clear signal Microsoft is building AI independence from OpenAI.

items.title: GLM 5.1 beats Claude Opus and GPT-5.4 on coding benchmark
items.body: Chinese firm Zhipu AI released GLM 5.1, an open-source LLM that tops the SWE-Bench Pro coding benchmark. Optimized for '8-hour workday' efficiency. Open-source competition from China is getting real.

ai_research.learn_today: Meta Muse Spark is the model to watch — a proprietary model built for social-scale deployment changes the competitive dynamics.

openclaw_agents.headline: MCP/LangGraph combo dominates agent production, IBM MCP webinar Apr 16, agents market projected $52B by 2030

items.title: MCP is the de facto tool standard — now Linux Foundation governed
items.body: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is the universal 'USB port for AI agents' — standardized connections to tools, data, and APIs across any model. Now backed by the Linux Foundation with 50+ enterprise partners including OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, AWS, and Salesforce. This isn't optional anymore, it's infrastructure.
items.try_this: If your OpenClaw agents connect to external tools, check whether they use MCP-compatible servers.

items.title: LangGraph + MCP = production agent stack
items.body: LangGraph (126k+ GitHub stars) is the leading orchestration framework for stateful multi-agent systems. It models workflows as cyclic graphs with conditional routing, persistence, and human-in-the-loop support. The LangGraph-MCP adapter combo is the top recommendation for enterprise agent deployment in 2026.
items.try_this: Kanerika's Apr 2 LangGraph-MCP integration guide is worth bookmarking for production setups.

items.title: IBM MCP Interoperability Webinar — April 16
items.body: IBM is hosting a webinar on MCP interoperability across enterprise systems. Worth attending if you're building multi-tool agent architectures. Expected to cover production pain points like tool overload and standardization gaps.

openclaw_agents.try_today: Review the LangGraph-MCP integration guide — it's the blueprint for scaling multi-tool agent workflows in production.

tesla.note: TSLA at $345.62, holding above 2026 lows. Q1 earnings April 22 — consensus expects EPS of -$0.24. JPMorgan bearish note warns of 60% downside. But Tesla reviving cheaper EV plans including a new compact SUV. FSD v14.3 rolling out. ~60 Cybercabs spotted at Giga Texas. Possible Roadster reveal this month.

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: SpaceX IPO is the dominant story. Confidentially filed April 1, targeting $1.5–1.75T valuation with $40–80B raise, potentially as soon as June. Bank of America, Goldman, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley leading. Up to 30% of shares allocated to retail investors. Starship Flight 12 delayed to early May. Successful Falcon 9 launch Apr 6 deploying 25 Starlink sats.
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: Grok 5 still not released — Q1 window missed. Musk says 'Grok 4.20 in ~3 weeks, Grok 5 in a few months' (from late 2025). Current model is Grok 4.1 (Nov 2025). xAI acquired by SpaceX in Feb 2026. Grok Imagine API (video gen) launched Jan 2026. Prediction markets (Kalshi, Manifold) betting on mid-2026 release.

items.title: Outcome
items.note: Keanu Reeves as a Hollywood star blackmailed into redemption by confronting those he's wronged. Co-stars Cameron Diaz, Matt Bomer, Jonah Hill, and Martin Scorsesse. Star-studded, premise sounds sharp. Drops today.

items.title: Christy
items.note: Oscar-bait biopic of boxer Christy Martin — tackles abuse, closeted identity, and survival in a brutal sport. Stars Sydney Sweeney, Ben Foster, Merritt Wever. Heavy but well-reviewed.

items.title: Thrash
items.note: Category 5 hurricane unleashes sharks. Stars Phoebe Dynevor and Djimon Hounsou. Shark-nado territory but Netflix is banking on it. Check reviews first.

items.title: The Miniature Wife
items.note: Comedy about a bestselling author shrunk to six inches tall by her husband's experiment. Stars Elizabeth Banks and Matthew Macfadyen. Absurd premise, strong cast. Could be a fun watch.

items.title: Sirat
items.note: Road drama — father and son searching for a missing daughter at a Moroccan rave. Stars Sergi López. Niche, could be great or pretentious. Wait for reviews.

items.title: HubSpot Breeze AI shifts to outcome-based pricing April 14
items.body: $0.50 per resolved customer conversation (65% resolution rate), $1 per qualified lead for the Prospecting Agent. This is the future — pay for results, not seats. Important precedent for any AI SaaS pricing strategy, including QuizzyDots and InstaClaw.

items.title: Agentic AI tools flooding martech — 12 new launches this week
items.body: Clarvos (autonomous campaign agents), Kustomer AI Signals (predictive behavior triggers), AI Digital Elevate (cross-channel neural analysis), and Basis Technologies (agentic media strategy). The martech stack is going fully agentic. Human-in-the-loop is becoming human-ON-the-loop.

items.title: Semrush: Human content still dominates top search ranks (80.5%)
items.body: Despite AI hype, 80.5% of top search results are human-written. AI helps with speed and productivity but doesn't replace voice and authenticity. Good news for anyone writing real content for QuizzyDots.

macro.mood: Friday morning calm. BTC at $72K, strongest crypto week in a month. S&P closed at 6,824.66 Thursday (+0.62%). CPI data today could move markets. TSLA flat ahead of April 22 earnings. SpaceX IPO filings dominating financial news.
macro.btc_friday_note: BTC at $72K+ heading into the weekend — strongest week in a month. Watch for weekend liquidity dips.
